subject: Chemistry
title: The Briggs-Rauscher Oscillation
description: A 60-second lesson on The Briggs-Rauscher Oscillation.

hook.text: A clear liquid turns amber, then deep blue, then clear again—and keeps cycling for several minutes.

buildup.text: The Briggs-Rauscher reaction mixes iodate, peroxide, and malonic acid. Three reactions fight for control.

discovery.text: Iodine forms and disappears in a chemical tug-of-war. Each color represents a different dominant reaction.

twist.text: It's a chemical clock. The oscillations are so regular you could almost set your watch by them.

climax.text: Oscillating reactions prove chemistry isn't always one-directional. Systems can cycle like a pendulum.

punchline.text: Clear, amber, blue, repeat. Chemistry that tells time.

quiz.question: What causes the color changes in the Briggs-Rauscher reaction?
options: Iodine forming and disappearing as competing reactions alternate
Different gases being released in sequence
Temperature fluctuations changing the liquid's color
